era: Hip-Hop. E=7 -- maximum energy. Cultural magnitude. #1 national hit. Grammy. Founded Nastymix Records. Scene architect. Third E=7 (175, 176, 179). Nastymix trilogy culmination (177-179). v1.49.319.

location.formative: Central District hip-hop scene. Built Nastymix Records infrastructure. Capitol Hill ('Posse on Broadway').
location.career: Sir Mix-a-Lot (Anthony Ray). Nastymix Records (founded 1985). Def American. Rhyme Cartel Records (founded). 1985-present.

members.name: Sir Mix-a-Lot
members.note: Central District. Founded Nastymix Records (1985). 'Baby Got Back' #1 for 5 weeks. Grammy (1993). 'Posse on Broadway' (1988).

associated_musicians.name: Kid Sensation
associated_musicians.relationship: Nastymix Records artist. Featured on 'Posse on Broadway.' Protege. Degree 177.

associated_musicians.name: High Performance
associated_musicians.relationship: Nastymix Records group from Tacoma. Signed by Mix-a-Lot's label. Degree 178.

associated_musicians.name: Rick Rubin
associated_musicians.relationship: Def American Records. Produced/distributed 'Baby Got Back.' Major-label bridge.

associated_musicians.name: Ed Locke
associated_musicians.relationship: Co-founder of Nastymix Records. Business partner.

theory_nodes.concept: Cultural magnitude as E=7 -- #1 national hit + label founder + scene architect + Grammy + cultural icon + four-decade career. The engine's highest energy level. Maximum.
examples: 'Baby Got Back' #1 for five weeks (1992). 10M+ copies.
Founded Nastymix Records (1985). First PNW hip-hop label.
'Posse on Broadway' (1988). Seattle hip-hop founding anthem.
Library of Congress National Recording Registry.
theory_nodes.description: Cultural magnitude. E=7 through total scene impact.

genealogy.relationship: Sir Mix-a-Lot at 179. Maximum energy. Scene architect.

theory_nodes.concept: Nastymix trilogy culmination (177-179) -- three-degree label arc building from protege (177) through regional bridge (178) to founder (179). Energy escalation: E=6, E=6, E=7.
theory_nodes.description: Nastymix trilogy culminates. The architect arrives last.

theory_nodes.concept: Three E=7 entries in five degrees (175-179) -- DoNormaal (radical transformation), Maliibu Miitch (uncompromising intensity), Sir Mix-a-Lot (cultural magnitude). Energy escalation toward halfway mark.
examples: Three modes of maximum energy in five degrees.
theory_nodes.description: E=7 concentration. Three maximum-energy entries near halfway mark.

genealogy.relationship: Sir Mix-a-Lot at 179. Third E=7. Cultural magnitude mode.

cross_references.relationship: NASTYMIX PROTEGE! Mix-a-Lot signed Kid Sensation. 'Posse on Broadway' connected them. Founder meets flagship MC. Trilogy degrees 177→179.
NASTYMIX SIGNED! Mix-a-Lot's label signed Tacoma's High Performance. Label bridged Seattle and Tacoma. Trilogy degrees 178→179.
SEATTLE HIP-HOP LINEAGE! Mix-a-Lot (179) to Macklemore (180). The two biggest Seattle hip-hop names in consecutive degrees. Pioneer to modern. Pre-halfway to halfway.
E=7 TRIO! DoNormaal (175) + Maliibu Miitch (176) + Sir Mix-a-Lot (179). Three maximum-energy entries in five degrees.
INDEPENDENT SUCCESS! Lewis/Macklemore (160) achieved independent #1 like Mix-a-Lot. Two eras of Seattle independent success at the top of charts.
